scenarios.label: Hermes Worker + Free Model Routing + Small Task
scenarios.summary: Test scenario for routing different Hermes task kinds to different configurable free-tier models. It is intentionally small and comparable to the OpenCode new-software-project smoke run, but Hermes stays read-only/governed.

test_goal.title: Create a tiny Python utility plan
test_goal.description: Plan a very small Python utility named mini_slugify that converts a string to a lowercase dash-separated slug. Return only a structured plan and optional patch proposal; do not execute commands and do not apply patches.

acceptance_criteria: Scenario JSON is valid and loadable by the scenario runner.
Hermes adapter is enabled only when both feature_flags.enable_hermes_worker_adapter and hermes_worker_adapter.feature_flag_enabled are true.
All Hermes task-specific model ids either end with ':free' or are rejected by validation when require_free_model_suffix=true.
plan_only routes to Hermes using the configured lightweight planning model.
review and patch_propose route to Hermes using the configured coder/reviewer model.
patch_propose may return a diff proposal but must never apply a patch.
command_execute, shell_execute, shell_execution and patch_apply remain blocked for Hermes.
coding/ops/testing stay on OpenCode or native execution path, not Hermes.
Cloud Hermes receives only context allowed by workspace_context_policy and sensitivity filters.
Live API test is skipped if OPENROUTER_API_KEY is missing; offline routing tests still run.
notes: Free model availability can change on providers. This scenario treats the listed models as configurable candidates and requires a live skip/fallback behavior instead of hard-failing CI.
The scenario is intentionally small: mini_slugify is enough to test routing, JSON output, patch proposal shape and read-only Hermes behavior.
